rationale: Both correctly diagnose the run-level/dummy-weight issue and recommend --run-level=full_model, matching the thread's actual resolution. Y is more tightly grounded: it explicitly ties in PR #4354 (mirroring akshatvishu's comment verbatim), explains why the full_model pytest marker doesn't override the --run-level default, correctly notes A100 is sufficient (matching yenuo26's actual A100 verification), and states the 'invalid' disposition explicitly. X instead claims the test 'requires H100 or MI325,' which contradicts the ground truth's A100 verification, and omits the PR #4354 link entirely, making it slightly less grounded and complete than Y.
